body_txt: A baby rabbit, even one that has never encountered a bird before, will still cower at the sight of a hawk's shadow. Some fears are nearly-universal, or just quite wide-spread. The dark , heights , blood , enclosed spaces , fire, snakes , spiders , killers , loud sounds , pain, death , monsters, humiliation, loneliness , being followed , complete emptiness , existential dread , gore , giants, being Eaten Alive ; these fears have always been with us. They are the dangers our early ancestors faced, and their shadows still haunt our nightmares . Most people are a little nervous about such things — not many people could walk on a glass bridge over the Grand Canyon without any railings, and not feel a little anxious — and full-blown phobias are easily enough induced. Naturally, writers of Horror fiction like to exploit these . See also: Why Did It Have to Be Snakes? , Snakes Are Sinister , Scaled Up , Spiders Are Scary , Scary Scorpions , Creepy Cockroach , Creepy Centipedes , Bears Are Bad News , Fangs Are Evil , Eye Scream , And I Must Scream , Jump Scare , Enclosed Space (which isn't necessarily this trope), Dark Is Evil (primal fears of darkness are the reason this trope is so prevalent), and a fair number of Horror Tropes .
